title: The Vertex
description: The last village. The shopkeeper who is just the idea of one. The traveler who has walked between worlds. The question.

steps.text: The village materialized slowly, as though it were being remembered rather than built. The Vertex was barely there — an edge case the universe had not quite resolved.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Welcome. I sell things. I think. The concept of commerce is... thinner here than it used to be.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: This is everyone. I was a shopkeeper in every world. Ironclad Arms. Ye Olde Medieval Surplus. The gear merchant. The company store. I'm still a shopkeeper. But now I'm just... the idea of one.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: I can offer you something that is exactly what you need. I just can't tell you what it is, because here, what you need can only be known by you.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: You outlasted everything. Every world, every boss, every system. The Calibrant threw six worlds at you and you broke all of them. Now there's nothing left to throw.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Now you go talk to them. And they ask you their question. And depending on your answer, this either ends or it doesn't.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Every party that has made it this far has heard the question. No two have answered it the same way. And every answer has been right.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: The question is not the kind that has wrong answers. Wrong answers are for systems. This is not a system. This is what the Calibrant had before they built the systems. And then forgot they had.
